reasoning: Gabon had 0 'Explosions/Remote violence' events over the 360 days before August 17, 2025 (indicated by the current reference value 1.000). To meet the resolution criteria, there must be at least 11 such events within a single 30-day window leading up to the target date. ACLED defines this category as incidents involving artillery, missile strikes, IEDs, airstrikes, or other remote mass-destructive weapons. The country is completely peaceful and relatively wealthy. A coup occurred in August 2023, but it was almost entirely bloodless, and the country transitioned to a presidential election in April 2025, which military leader Nguema easily won. There is no active insurgency, no jihadist presence, and no organized armed rebel groups in Gabon. It is virtually impossible for a country to suddenly experience 11+ remote violence/explosion events within a 30-day period without an ongoing civil war or active conflict. Given the closest target date is 2025-08-24 (which is almost fully overlapping with the peaceful past 30 days) and later dates extend to Feb 2026 without any indications of armed conflict forming, the probability remains solidly at the absolute minimal floor. I give a slightly higher baseline to later dates logically due to longer horizon, but the probability is practically zero.
